publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-03 00:18:35 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Reports, correspondence, memoranda, proposals, case studies, handwritten notes, papers, books, booklets, transparencies, DVDs, slide shows, and other digital material focused on housing programs and community land trust corporations, publications and technical writing, lectures, speeches, and interviews, courses, workshops, and biographical files of the scholar and community land trust activist John Emmeus Davis (b. 1949), document his professional action, teaching, and writing, dating from 1968 to 2022.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Emmeus Davis (b. 1949) is a scholar, community activist, and organizer best known for his contributions to the understanding and development of community land trusts (CTL). His professional practice, teaching, and writing focused on assisting municipal governments, community land trusts, and other NGOs in developing policies and programs for the creation of housing that remains permanently affordable. He earned his B.A. in Philosophy from Vanderbilt University in 1971, an M.S. in Developmental Sociology from Cornell University in 1981, and a Ph.D. in Community Development Planning and Community and Regional Sociology from Cornell in 1986. He is the co-founder and board president of the Center for Community Land Trust Innovation (2018) and editor-in-chief of Terra Nostra Press (2018-now). He was also a partner in Burlington Associates in Community Development (1993). Davis helped establish the National CTL Academy in 2006 and served four years as the Academy's Dean. Between 1986 and 1996, he was the city's housing director in Burlington, Vermont, under the Mayors Bernie Sanders and Peter Clavel. Earlier, he worked at the Institute for Community Economics and in Appalachia as a community organizer, social worker, and director of a social services program. Davis is the author, co-author, and editor of several publications. He has drafted municipal ordinances for inclusionary zoning, housing trust funds, and restrictions on the demolition and conversion of housing. He is the co-author of the Community Land Trust Handbook (Rodale Press, 1982) and the author of the "community land trusts" definition enacted into federal law in 1992. He is also the co-producer of the film "Arch of Justice: The Rise, Fall, and Rebirth of a Beloved Community (2016) and technical advisor in the films "Streets of Dreams: Development without Displacement in Communities of Color," and, "Home and Hands: Community Land Trusts in Action." For his contributions to advancing the cause of CLTs, he received the Swann-Matthei Award from the National CLT Network in 2006.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Emmeus Davis papers, #8815. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The John Emmeus Davis papers document the professional action, teaching, and writing of the scholar and community land trust activist John Emmeus Davis (b. 1949), dating from 1968 to 2022. The collection consists of reports, correspondence, memoranda, proposals, case studies, handwritten notes, papers, books and booklets, transparencies, DVDs, slide shows, and other digital material focused on housing programs and community land trust corporations, publications and technical writing, lectures, speeches, and interviews of John E. Davis, relevant courses, workshops, and biographical files. Documents cover the mission, programs, and policies of the City of Burlington, Vermont's Community and Economic Development Office (CEDO), with a particular focus on the housing division headed by John Emmeus Davis for ten years, as well as the formation and operation of the Burlington Community Land Trust (BCLT), renamed the Champlain Housing Trust in 2006 after a merger of BCLT and the Lake Champlain Housing Development Corporation (1984-2021). The material also pertains to several case studies, in alphabetical order of technical assistance to national, international, and local NGOs and municipal or state governments (1983-2022). The collection also includes books, manuals, reports, and articles authored, co-authored, edited, or co-edited by John Emmeus Davis, including the various blogs written for Shelterforce Magazine's online journal, Rooflines; speeches, interviews, and lectures; training, workshops, and graduate-level college courses taught by John Emmeus Davis, focused on housing policy and neighborhood planning. The digital material includes several relevant films and slide shows, and the collection overlaps significantly with manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frequent abbreviations: Burlington Community & Economic Development Office (CEDO), Community Land Trust (CLT).

did.unittitle: Davis, John Emmerus leaving the National CLT Academy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an autobiographical sketch for the website of the Appalachian Student Health Coalition (2015), a Master List of Publications, and a Master List of Consultancies. See also: Series V. Digital Material, folder 32. JEDavis biographical-JEDfolder.

did.unittitle: Series II. Housing Programs and Corporations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frequent abbreviations: Burlington Community & Economic Development Office (CEDO), Burlington Community Land Trust (BCLT), Land Trust Collaborative (LTC), Community Land Trust (CLT), John E. Davis (JED).

did.unittitle: NeighborWorks America: Redesign of criteria and process for national grantmaking to local affiliates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Burlington Associates was hired to plan and execute a participatory planning process that included NeighborWorks EDs and regional directors across the USA. BA then drafted recommendations for revamping the process of grantmaking for local NWA affiliates. John Davis and Mary O'Hara played the leading roles in this year-long project.

Burlington Community Land Trust (BCLT)/Champlain Housing Trust (CHL).
Video: Homes & Hands: CLTs in Action

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Burlington Community Land Trust is one of three featured CLTs in this video.

did.unittitle: A New Way Home. Produced by NCB Capital Impact and NeighborWorks America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CHT is one of the organizations featured in this documentary.

did.unittitle: Champlain Housing Trust. Produced by Building and Social Housing Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video profile created to document the winner of the the 2008 World Habitat Award.

did.unittitle: Video: A CCTV interview with John Davis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After planning and staffing the Old North End Enterprise Community, JED retired from CEDO. He was interviewed by CCTV in Burlington Vermont, looking back at his 10 years working in City Hall.

did.unittitle: Video: Arc of Justice: The Rise, Fall, and Rebirth of a Beloved Community

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20-minute documentary co-produced by JED, featuring New Communities Inc., widely recognized as having been the first modern-day CLT. The story traces NCI's origins in the Civil Rights Movement, the loss of the CLT's land, and the historic suit against USDA.

did.unittitle: Video: Streets of Dreams: Development without Displacement in Communities of Color

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video features CLTs in African-American neighborhoods in Atlanta GA, Delray Beach FL, and New Orleans, LA.

did.unittitle: Slide Show: Common Ground: An Introduction to CLTs. Produced by ICE. 1986

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earliest multi-media production describing the CLT. It was commissioned by the Institute for Community Economics. At the center of the story is the Community land Cooperative of Cincinnati, although the Burlington CLT is also featured.

did.unittitle: Slide Show: Common Ground: An Introduction to CLTs. Produced by ICE. 1986

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Video for which JED served as a technical adviser features CLTs in Durham NC, Albuquerque NM, and Burlington VT.

did.unittitle: Slide Show: Roots of the CLT (PPT slides and narration by JED)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a four-part history of the origins of the CLT model and movement in the USA, written, narrated, and recorded by JED. See also Series IV, Folder 35. for copies the PPT slides and the scripts in both English and Spanish written by JED for this presentation.

did.unittitle: Arc of Justice. Notes, New Communities Inc. history & viewers guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Frequent abbreviations: John E. Davis (JED).

scopecontent.p: 10 documents; 2 images; includes two agricultural ground leases

scopecontent.p: 1 document: profile of the ACLT, Albany NY; 8 images; 1 logo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 documents; 1 folder of photos containing 30 images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 documents, including an ALT profile; 1 folder of photos, containing 25 images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 24 documents, including agenda for planning committee, original ALTC blueprint from 2009, and strategic plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 documents, most pertaining to JED's speaking and training in Australia in 2009; 5 images; includes articles by Louise Crabtree.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 CLT-related images created by artist Bonnie Acker, including multiple covers for Terra Nostra Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 27 documents; 2 folders: "Stewardship," containing 9 docs and "DNI-DSNI profile," containing 41 images and 2 documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 documents related to Thistle Community Housing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 documents; 2 images; 1 folder containing 5 documents related to 2018 training for favela leaders in Rio, 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 folders, containing photographs from JED's trips to Brussels in 2011, 2014, and 2018, supporting development of the Brussels CLT; 1 folder containing three profiles of the Brussels CLT.

did.unittitle: Burlington Community Land Trust, Champlain Housing Trust, & Community and Economic Development Office-JED

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 documents; 20 images; 2 PPT presentations; 4 folders: "World Habitat Award, 2018," containing 3 documents and 10 images; "CHT Profile"; and "Performance evaluations," containing three publications; and Burlington Community and Economic Office, containing 5 images, 5 docs, and 2 pdfs. See also: I.A. Burlington Community & Economic Development Office (CEDO), Progressive Housing Policy, and Enterprise Community and I.B. Burlington Community Land Trust (BCLT)/ Champlain Housing Trust (CHL).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 documents; 1 folder: "World CLT Day, 2021." See also: Box 2, Folder 30 and the CLT Chronology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to creation of the Chicago CLT and subsequent planning for the CLT's programs. See also: Box 2, Folder 54-57.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to TA to Ohio City Near West, the Cleveland Foundation's attempts to establish a CLT, and creation of the Hough CLT. See also: Box 2, Folder 60-62.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organizational documents, training materials, and images from training events convened by the National CLT Academy, a chartered program of the National CLT Network, which operated from 2006 to 2012. Included is a gallery of 44 photos from Academy trainings. See also: Box 2, Folder 34.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochures, agendas, and photographs from national and regional CLT conferences in the USA. Included is a gallery of 111 photos from CLT conferences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 non-academic articles about CLTs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Proof of Concept" paper by Patterson and Dyson, laying the foundation for CLT development in the UK.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to the formation and operation of the CLT Network in the USA, before becoming Grounded Solutions Network. See also: Box 2, Folder 35.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On October 2, 2018, the day before the Grounded Solutions Conference in Pittsburgh, 50 CLT leaders from across the country were invited to a day-long charette to assess strengths and weaknesses of CLTs in the USA and to lay plans for the future. (This was the last time that CLT peers gathered separately from all the other organizations and municipalities invited to conferences held by the Grounded Solutions Network.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 46 scholarly articles (not authored by JED) about community land trusts around the world.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents and images from the 1985 slide show, Common Ground, and two folders containing materials related to making and publicizing the documentary films: Homes&Hands and Arc of Justice. The latter contains a folder of photos and materials from New Communities, Inc., the first CLT (circa 1970-75).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 documents related to the formation and operation of the Diamond State CLT in Delaware the only state-wide CLT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recommendations for improving Denver's IZ program, an assessment of feasibility of a three-CLT merger, a "Feasibility Study & Business Plan for a Proposed Community Land Trust Program Serving Denver's Globeville, Elyria and Swansea Neighborhoods," and "Evaluating the Potential for Establishing or Expanding Community Land Trust Programs in Eight Colorado County." See also Box 3, Folder 7-9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 documents and 3 folders: Delray Beach CLT, Bahama Conch CLT (Key West), and South Florida Smart Growth CLT. See also: Box 3, Folder 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 photos, 1 document, and copies of slides from JED's trainings in Ghent, Belgium, 2014.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 documents helping GSN to define what a "community of interest" might be -- an entity named (but not defined) in GSN's bylaws. See also: Box 3, Folder 35.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents, PPT presentations, and the 2017 Shelter Report. For a printed copy of Shelter Report, see Box 3, Folder 76.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 documents and 1 image related to the Heritage Lands Initiative, a chartered program of the National CLT Network.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 22 documents related to ICE, plus a folder of 42 photos of people and events related to ICE. See also: Box 2, Folder 33.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.E. Davis resume, 310-word bio, master list of publications, master list of training and technical assistance, folder containing 11 posters advertising JED talks, and folder containing 23 photographs of JED from trainings and presentations. Included, as well, is a folder containing documents for Burlington Associates in Community Development LLC, a consulting cooperative co-founded by JED. See also: Box 3, Folder 74.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five interviews given by JED. See also: Box 5, Folder 8, video interview from 2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: PPT slides, pdfs, and notes for 38 presentations by JED. Included is a folder containing five sets of PPT slides and both English and Spanish scripts for a four-part series on Roots of the CLT, produced, narrated, and recorded by JED in 2013. See Box 5, Folder 8, for a DVD copy of this narrated presentation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital copies of 24 publications authored by JED, plus a folder containing copies of 16 blogs written by JED for Shelterforce. See also: Series II.A. Publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Digital copies of 18 performance evaluations, feasibility studies, and program designs, authored or co-authored by JED. See also Box 3, Folders 29. "Home Equity Living Plan." See also: Series V. Digital Material (folder 46) for copies of two 3-year strategic plans written by JED for the Philadelphia Women's Community Revitalization Project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 photographs taken during 2014 tour of Letchworth Garden City in 2014. Two publications about Garden Cities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: PPT slides (in pdf format) from training with Leuvin's new mayor and housing staff, laying the foundation for a Leuvin CLT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: PPT slides and photo from 2018 presentation at Lille, France and notes from zoom presentation for Lille gathering in 2021.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, slides, and notes from presentations in Liverpool, England, 2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 photographs from lecture given at the London CLTs AGM in 2011 and from a seminar given at the first AGM of the CLT Network (UK). Also includes two articles about the London CLT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 files related to JED's assistance to a group of five CLTs in LA County to create a blueprint for collaboration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assistance to the Madison Area CLT began in the 1990s and continued through the transition from Sol Levin, MACLT's founding ED, to Greg Rosenberg, to Olivia Williams. Photos and files in this digital folder are from 2008-2020. See also Box 3, Folders 29-32.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two documents from start-up of the Island Housing Trust.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two folders, one containing documents related to the Community Home Trust in Chapel Hill and the other related to two attempts to establish a CLT in Asheville.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For over a dozen years, JED has assisted the Women's Community Revitalization Project (WCRP) in its efforts to establish the Community Justice Land Trust and to win support from the City of Philadelphia and the Philadelphia Land Bank for CLT developments throughout Philadelphia. Contents include WCRP's 2015-2018 Strategic Plan and WCRP's 2019-2022 Strategic Plan, both of them facilitated and written by JED. See also: Box 3, Folders 65-70.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 documents related to start-up of the Portland CLT (later re-named Proud Ground).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two folders, one for the Lopez CLT and one for the OPAL ("Of Land and People") CLT. Each folder contains organizational profiles, organizational documents, newspaper articles, and two dozen photographs.

did.unittitle: San Juan PR-Cano Martin Pena CLT-JED

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 photographs from a 2008 visit to San Juan, Puerto Rico, in support of ENLACE and the Cano Martin Pena CLT. Folder also contains a nomination for World Habitat Award and an essay about the CLT's development. See also: Box 3, Folder 53.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials prepared for posting on the website of the Sanders Institute.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 documents related to assistance to the State College CLT.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents and images sorted into three folders, each devoted to a different publishing project of Terra Nostra Press: On Common Ground; selected Monographs (including full Spanish text for La Inseguridad; Community Matter. See also: Box 2, Folder 30.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Profiles of the Valley CLT, along with organizational documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 34 documents and images related to the Woodland CLT in the Appalachian region of east Tennessee. Included, as well, is a profile of Marie Cirillo, authored by JED in 2015, which appears on the website of the Appalachian Student Health Coalition.
